title: LaplacianWords
summary: Defines words built from ambient Laplacians and multiplication, together with total-power bookkeeping.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines words built from ambient Laplacians and multiplication, together with total-power bookkeeping.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/LaplacianWords.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A rank-r Laplacian monomial word. Its mathematical data are: output Power: the natural numbers; input Power: Fin r maps to the natural numbers.
correspondence.leanType: structure LaplacianWord (r : ℕ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The product of input-side Laplacian powers. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, w of type laplacian word r, calconf of type calculus conf, and f of type cal conf.multi input r, its defining expression is the function sending U to ∏ i : Fin r, Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian W.input Power i F i U.
correspondence.leanType: def inputProduct {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The rank-r multi-operator represented by a Laplacian word. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, w of type laplacian word r, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is the function sending F to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian W.output Power W.input Product Cal Conf F.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Total Laplacian degree in the word. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and w of type laplacian word r, its defining expression is W.output Power plus ∑ i : Fin r, W.input Power i.
correspondence.leanType: def totalPower {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) : ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expected output weight from applying the word to inputs of multiweight w. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, w of type laplacian word r, calconf of type calculus conf, and w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, its defining expression is Cal Conf.total Weight w minus 2 times W.total Power.
correspondence.leanType: def expectedOutputWeight {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, W of type Laplacian Word r, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, W.input Product Cal Conf F U equals ∏ i : Fin r, Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian W.input Power i F i U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;W\colon \operatorname{LaplacianWord} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad W.\operatorname{inputProduct} \operatorname{CalConf} F U = \prod i \colon \operatorname{Fin} r, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} (W.\operatorname{inputPower} i) (F i) U
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputProduct_apply {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : W.inputProduct CalConf F U = ∏ i : Fin r, CalConf.lapPow (W.inputPower i) (F i) U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, W of type Laplacian Word r, and F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, W.to Operator Cal Conf F equals Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian W.output Power W.input Product Cal Conf F.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;W\colon \operatorname{LaplacianWord} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\quad W.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} W.\operatorname{outputPower} (W.\operatorname{inputProduct} \operatorname{CalConf} F)
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : W.toOperator CalConf F = CalConf.lapPow W.outputPower (W.inputProduct CalConf F)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Abstract product homogeneity identity. This is the isolated assumption needed to prove weight mapping for Laplacian words. Later it can be proved from an X_mul / Leibniz rule. Its mathematical data are: product homogeneous: for every r : the natural numbers A : Fin r maps to Function Conf w : Fin r maps to the real numbers, for every i : Fin r, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w i A i maps to Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous Cal Conf.total Weight w the function sending U to ∏ i : Fin r, A i U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{product\_homogeneous}&\colon \forall {r \colon \mathbb{N}} (A \colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}) (w \colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R}), (\forall i \colon \operatorname{Fin} r, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (w i) (A i)) \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{totalWeight} w) (U \longmapsto \prod i \colon \operatorname{Fin} r, A i U)\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure ProductHomogeneityIdentities : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The input product has the sum of the shifted input weights. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;Hprod\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{ProductHomogeneityIdentities},\;r\colon \mathbb{N},\;W\colon \operatorname{LaplacianWord} r,\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;hF\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsMultiHomogeneous} w F,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{totalWeight} (\operatorname{fun} i \colon \operatorname{Fin} r => w i - 2 \cdot W.\operatorname{inputPower} i )) (W.\operatorname{inputProduct} \operatorname{CalConf} F)
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputProduct_mapsWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hF : CalConf.IsMultiHomogeneous w F) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.totalWeight (fun i : Fin r => w i - 2 * (W.inputPower i : ℝ))) (W.inputProduct CalConf F)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A Laplacian word maps its input multiweight to its expected output weight. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : CalConf.MapsWeights (W.toOperator CalConf) w (W.expectedOutputWeight CalConf w)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The rank-two word (a; b, c), i.e. Δ̃^a(Δ̃^b f · Δ̃^c g). In explicit terms, for a, b, c in the natural numbers, its defining expression is output Power : equals a input Power : equals the function sending i to if i equals 0 then b else c.
correspondence.leanType: def mk (a b c : ℕ) : LaplacianWord 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, mk a b c.output Power equals a.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_outputPower (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).outputPower = a

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, mk a b c.input Power 0 equals b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_zero (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).inputPower 0 = b

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, mk a b c.input Power 1 equals c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_one (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).inputPower 1 = c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, mk a b c.total Power equals a plus b plus c.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,a,b,c\colon \mathbb{N},\quad (\operatorname{mk} a b c).\operatorname{totalPower} = a + b + c
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalPower (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).totalPower = a + b + c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, and w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, mk a b c.expected Output Weight Cal Conf Cal Conf.bi Weights w₁ w₂ equals w₁ plus w₂ minus 2 times a plus b plus c : the real numbers.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,a,b,c\colon \mathbb{N},\;w₁,w₂\colon \mathbb{R},\quad (\operatorname{mk} a b c).\operatorname{expectedOutputWeight} \operatorname{CalConf} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{biWeights} w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂ - 2 \cdot (a + b + c \colon \mathbb{R})
correspondence.leanType: theorem expectedOutputWeight (a b c : ℕ) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : (mk a b c).expectedOutputWeight CalConf (CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂ - 2 * ((a + b + c : ℕ) : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c in the natural numbers, and f, g of type Function Conf, mk a b c.to Operator Cal Conf Cal Conf.pair Input f g equals Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian a the function sending U to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian b f U times Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian c g U : equals by unfold Laplacian Word.to Operator Laplacian Word.input Product mk congr 1 funext U rw Fin.prod univ two simp pair Input.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,a,b,c\colon \mathbb{N},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad (\operatorname{mk} a b c).\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{pairInput} f g) = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} a (U \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} b f U \cdot \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} c g U) = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{unfold} \operatorname{LaplacianWord}.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{LaplacianWord}.\operatorname{inputProduct} \operatorname{mk} \operatorname{congr} 1 \operatorname{funext} U \operatorname{rw} [\operatorname{Fin}.\operatorname{prod\_univ\_two}] \operatorname{simp} [\operatorname{pairInput}]
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_pairInput_apply (a b c : ℕ) (f g : Function Conf) : (mk a b c).toOperator CalConf (CalConf.pairInput f g) = CalConf.lapPow a (fun U => CalConf.lapPow b f U * CalConf.lapPow c g U) := by unfold LaplacianWord.toOperator LaplacianWord.inputProduct mk congr 1 funext U rw [Fin.prod_univ_two] simp [pairInput]

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The rank-three word (a; b, c, d). In explicit terms, for a, b, c, d in the natural numbers, its defining expression is output Power : equals a input Power : equals the function sending i to if i equals 0 then b else if i equals 1 then c else d.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{mk}(a,b,c,d)=\operatorname{outputPower} = a \operatorname{inputPower} = i \longmapsto \operatorname{if} i = 0 \operatorname{then} b \operatorname{else} \operatorname{if} i = 1 \operatorname{then} c \operatorname{else} d
correspondence.leanType: def mk (a b c d : ℕ) : LaplacianWord 3

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c, d in the natural numbers, mk a b c d.output Power equals a.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_outputPower (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).outputPower = a

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c, d in the natural numbers, mk a b c d.input Power 0 equals b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_zero (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 0 = b

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c, d in the natural numbers, mk a b c d.input Power 1 equals c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_one (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 1 = c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b, c, d in the natural numbers, mk a b c d.input Power 2 equals d.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_two (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 2 = d

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data for a finite bidifferential linear combination of Laplacian words. Its mathematical data are: support: Finset Laplacian Word 2; coeff: Laplacian Word 2 maps to the real numbers.
correspondence.leanType: structure BiLaplacianCombinationData

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The bidifferential operator associated to a finite Laplacian-word sum. In explicit terms, for d of type bi laplacian combination data, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.operator Sum D.support D.coeff the function sending W to W.to Operator Cal Conf.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (D : BiLaplacianCombinationData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For D of type Bi Laplacian Combination Data, F of type Cal Conf.Bi Input, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, D.to Operator Cal Conf F U equals ∑ W belongs to D.support, D.coeff W times W.to Operator Cal Conf F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,D\colon \operatorname{BiLaplacianCombinationData},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{BiInput},\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad D.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U = \sum W \in D.\operatorname{support}, D.\operatorname{coeff} W \cdot W.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply (D : BiLaplacianCombinationData) (F : CalConf.BiInput) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ W ∈ D.support, D.coeff W * W.toOperator CalConf F U

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
